analysis_metadata.title: Open-data proxy reinterpretation of same-charge leptons plus b-jets selections
analysis_metadata.reference_analysis_title: Search for new phenomena in events with same-charge leptons and b-jets in pp collisions at sqrt(s) = 13 TeV with the ATLAS detector
analysis_metadata.analysis_scope_note: This JSON intentionally describes what can be executed with the available ATLAS open-data ntuples. It preserves the same-charge dilepton/trilepton object and region structure, but does not request official VLQ mass limits, full CLs limits, fake-lepton matrix-method reproduction, or electron charge-misID calibration when the required signal grids and auxiliary inputs are absent.

plotting_policy.scope: Use for every produced, regenerated, modified, or reviewed figure and plot manifest.
plotting_policy.style_requirement: Apply ATLAS-style HEP plotting conventions while preserving selections, weights, binning, object definitions, region thresholds, sample roles, and blinding/unblinding policy.
plotting_policy.label_policy: Use open-data/diagnostic label text such as 'ATLAS Open Data' or 'ATLAS Open Data, diagnostic proxy'. Do not use 'ATLAS' or 'ATLAS Preliminary' as an approval label for unapproved local outputs.
required_outputs: true PDF for final analysis plots whenever practical
plot manifest recording file paths, backend, style package, label text, observed-data status, and diagnostic-only status
review_checks: data markers are black with uncertainty bars
central backgrounds and signal proxies use distinguishable HEP/ATLAS-style colors
negative signed MC yields are not hidden by cosmetics
report captions explain the open-data proxy scope without making official ATLAS claims

region_zero_yield_policy.scope: Required for every signal region with zero observed data, zero central background, zero central signal proxy, or a yield clipped from a negative signed MC sum.
audit_requirements: record observed-data, central-background, and central-signal-proxy weighted and unweighted yields
record raw signed MC yield separately from any nonnegative yield used by a counting statistic
identify whether the zero is expected from sparse open-data acceptance or suspicious from implementation state
for suspicious zeros, name the likely source: sample-scope mismatch, object-definition difference, branch fallback, signed-weight cancellation, missing region threshold, or parser error
summarize zero-yield regions in the final report
region_zero_yield_policy.acceptance_rule: A zero-yield region is acceptable only when the audit traces the result to the fixed region definition and available open-data sample acceptance, or explicitly blocks the affected claim.

analysis_objectives.target: same-charge dilepton and trilepton plus b-jets open-data three-top/four-top proxy analysis
signal_models: SM four-top production proxy (open-data samples 412043 and 412044)
BSM four-top scalar shape proxy (open-data sample 510097)
reported_outputs: control-proxy diagnostics for fake/non-prompt and charge-misID studies
diagnostic expected and observed counting-proxy sensitivity for three-top/four-top proxy samples
explicit list of blocked official VLQ/CLs/mass-limit claims

signal_signatures.production_mode: pair production of tier (1,1) states followed by cascades to A(1,1) -> ttbar

benchmark_scenarios: degenerate heavy scalar and pseudo-scalar H/A
signal_signatures.target_final_state_signature: same-sign dilepton or trilepton plus b-jets

signal_signatures.model: same-sign top-quark pair production in an FCNC dark-matter mediator model
signal_signatures.target_final_state_signature: exactly two positively charged leptons plus b-jets
signal_signatures.selection_specific_note: Only positively charged lepton pairs are used because tt production is much larger than tbar tbar production in pp collisions.
signal_signatures.open_data_status: reference-only unless matching local samples and model inputs are present

signal_signatures.target_final_state_signature: same-sign dilepton or trilepton plus b-jets from a top-rich final state

nuisance_parameter_classes.details: Control-sample definition, control-sample statistics, and prompt-subtraction normalization; dominant component typically around 40-50% of the fake/non-prompt yield in the signal regions.
About 20% of the expected charge-misID yield in the signal regions, plus an anti-correlated component from fake/non-prompt subtraction.

results.statement: This open-data target does not request official VLQ, four-top, or same-sign-top exclusions. It requests reproducible diagnostic yields and counting-proxy sensitivities using available three-top and four-top proxy samples.

results.blocking_reason: The required dedicated signal grids, full likelihood/nuisance model, fake-lepton matrix method, and charge-misID calibration inputs are not available in the open-data workspace.

allowed_outputs: central sample registry and sample exclusion reasons
cutflows with IO prefilters separated from physics cuts
signal-region yields for data, central backgrounds, and named three-top/four-top signal proxies
diagnostic expected sensitivity and observed count comparisons using a fixed simplified model

analysis_design_insights.statement: For the VLQ and four-top searches, the preselected sample is first split by lepton multiplicity (2l or 3l) and b-tag multiplicity (1, 2, or >=3), and then by HT and ETmiss requirements.

Signal regions with at least three b-tagged jets are further split: relaxed HT plus high jet multiplicity is used for four-top sensitivity, while high HT plus lower jet multiplicity enhances VLQ sensitivity.

VLQ/four-top regions use inclusive lepton flavours to increase the statistics of the loosely selected samples used for reducible-background estimation, whereas the same-sign top search splits ee, e mu, and mu mu because the looser optimized selection provides enough statistics.
The same-sign top search uses exactly two positive leptons, HT > 750 GeV, ETmiss > 40 GeV, at least one b-tagged jet, and |delta phi_ll| > 2.5.
Validation regions kinematically close to the signal regions are defined to test the background model; the smallest reported validation-region p-value is 0.10.

For the open-data proxy workflow, reducible fake/non-prompt and charge-misID backgrounds are not reproduced as official data-driven estimates; their regions are retained as diagnostics while central yields use available simulated groups with explicit caveats.
Statistical outputs are diagnostic counting proxies. Official CLs, mass limits, and coupling limits are blocked unless a dedicated signal grid and full nuisance/correlation model are added.

leptons.definition: entries in lep_* arrays with abs(lep_type) in {11,13}, nonzero charge, |eta| < 2.5, and sig_lep != 0 when available
leptons.quality_source: open-data sig_lep branch; tight/isolation/impact-parameter details are not re-derived unless sig_lep is unavailable

electrons.eta_requirement: open-data proxy uses |lep_eta| < 2.5 plus sig_lep; exact official electron crack-veto status is not separately resolvable from a dedicated branch

jets.overlap_removal: DeltaR(jet, selected lepton) > 0.4 computed from jet_phi/eta and lep_phi/eta

b_tagging.official_working_point_claim: ATLAS Open Data continuous DL1dv0 quantile convention: >=4 is the 70 percent working point; >=3 is 77 percent and >=5 is 60 percent

trigger.policy: open-data trigger approximation; exact official multilepton trigger strategy is not reproduced

same_charge_lepton_category.definition: If exactly two of the three highest-pT leptons satisfy the nominal criteria, they must have the same electric charge.

trilepton_category.definition: If the three highest-pT leptons satisfy the nominal criteria, no requirement is imposed on charge or pair invariant mass.

validation_regions.lepton_charge_requirement: e- e- or e- mu- or mu- mu-

retrieval_features.validation_region_rule: Events that appear in any signal region are vetoed in the validation regions.
background_estimation_tags: MC-modeled irreducible backgrounds when matching samples exist
statistical_method_tags: expected model fixed before observed count comparison
no official CLs exclusion without full likelihood inputs
sensitivity_drivers: jet multiplicity split in >=3 b-tag regions
same-sign dilepton and trilepton final states
large azimuthal separation between leptons in same-sign top search
signal_target_tags: reference VLQ topology without mass-limit claim
retrieval_features.fit_observable: event counts per signal region or approved family diagnostic

lepton_flavour_strategy.same_sign_tt: split into ee, e mu, and mu mu

sample_processing_policy.registered_scope: all readable ROOT event samples discovered under input-data/data and input-data/MC
sample_processing_policy.minimum_required_processing_scope: all observed data samples, all central diagnostic background samples, and the explicit central three/four-top proxy signal samples
sample_processing_policy.central_interpretation_scope: only allowlisted central backgrounds and the explicit central signal proxy samples may enter central diagnostic yields or statistics
sample_processing_policy.noncentral_samples: may be processed for audit, bookkeeping, or separate reinterpretation studies, but must be excluded from central diagnostic yields and statistics unless explicitly promoted by a revised analysis contract
required_accounting_artifacts: sample registry with every discovered ROOT sample
noncentral sample list with exclusion reason
per-sample raw tree entries and processed entries

central_signal_policy.primary_signal_proxy: available three-top and four-top open-data samples
central_signal_policy.interpretation_note: Three-top and four-top samples preserve the high-lepton-multiplicity, high-b-jet, top-rich event topology used by the VLQ regions, but they are not VLQ signal models.

noncentral_signal_alternative_policy.atlas_reference_behavior: A central ATLAS VLQ result would use dedicated VLQ signal grids for each mass and branching-ratio hypothesis. Other BSM samples are not merged into the VLQ signal model and are not Standard Model backgrounds.
noncentral_signal_alternative_policy.classification_rule: Any BSM, exotic, SUSY, Wprime, Zprime, leptoquark, dark-matter, or other signal-like MC sample not listed in central_proxy_sample_ids is a noncentral signal alternative.
allowed_use: process for sample accounting and diagnostic kinematic studies
run one model or DSID at a time as a separately labeled diagnostic reinterpretation
keep in noncentral sample artifacts with raw and selected-event counts
forbidden_use: merge into the central three/four-top signal proxy
merge into central Standard Model background yields
quote a combined VLQ signal result from heterogeneous BSM alternatives
promote to central signal without revising this analysis contract
noncentral_signal_alternative_policy.required_reporting: Reports must state that noncentral signal alternatives are excluded from central VLQ proxy yields/statistics and list their DSIDs or artifact path.

background_policy.irreducible_backgrounds: simulation-driven from available ttW, ttZ, ttH, diboson, four-top, and rare-top-like samples when present
background_policy.central_background_sample_policy: Use one central sample representation per physics background process or exclusive process slice. Generator, shower, radiation, pThard, Herwig/H7UE, ShowerSys, and diagram-subtraction alternatives are noncentral systematic or modeling alternatives unless explicitly promoted by a revised analysis contract.

noncentral_background_alternative_policy.classification_rule: Samples with pThard, Herwig, H7UE, ShowerSys, _shw, DS_dyn, or explicitly listed ttbar alternative DSIDs are processed for accounting and diagnostics but not added to central background yields.

explicit_noncentral_background_sample_id.411233: Powheg+Herwig7 ttbar single-lepton alternative to the central ttbar modeling
explicit_noncentral_background_sample_id.411234: Powheg+Herwig7 ttbar dilepton alternative to the central ttbar modeling
explicit_noncentral_background_sample_id.411316: Powheg+Herwig7 ttbar all-hadronic alternative to the central ttbar modeling
explicit_noncentral_background_sample_id.601491: Powheg+Pythia8 pThard ttbar dilepton radiation alternative
explicit_noncentral_background_sample_id.601495: Powheg+Pythia8 pThard ttbar all-hadronic radiation alternative
explicit_noncentral_background_sample_id.601497: Powheg+Pythia8 pThard ttbar single-lepton radiation alternative

noncentral_background_alternative_policy.central_yield_rule: Central background cut-flow, nominal stacked plots, and statistical inputs must sum only samples with central_sample true. Noncentral alternatives must be kept in audit artifacts with selected-event counts and exclusion reasons.
required_artifacts: outputs/report/data_mc_discrepancy_audit.json top-contributor and central-only cross-check when MC is materially above data
noncentral_background_alternative_policy.motivation: Alternative samples model the same physics process under a different generator, shower, radiation, or overlap-removal scheme. Adding them to the nominal process double counts the background and inflates expected yields.

background_policy.fake_nonprompt_method: not reproducible from open data; save control-proxy counts only
background_policy.electron_charge_misid_method: not reproducible from open data; save opposite-charge application-proxy diagnostics only
background_policy.central_background_uncertainty: flat conservative diagnostic uncertainty plus MC statistical uncertainty unless a richer nuisance model is provided

event_policy.nominal_lepton_requirement: at least two selected leptons before same-sign/trilepton categorization

baseline_multiagent_comparability_requir: same noncentral signal alternative exclusion policy
same raw tree entries versus processed entries semantics
open_data_execution_contract.branch_contract_summary: All 389 open-data files have the core reco branches needed for the proxy analysis, including the DL1dv0 continuous b-tag quantile and flavour-tagging scale-factor branches. Official auxiliary branches for fake, charge-flip, truth prompt/fake labels, and full nuisance modeling are absent.

open_data_particle_definitions.leptons: Use the ntuple-provided signal-lepton mask `sig_lep` when present; require pT > 28 GeV, |eta| < 2.5, nonzero charge, and type 11 or 13. The open-data branch mask is treated as the authoritative tight/isolation/impact-parameter signal-lepton definition.
open_data_particle_definitions.electrons: Electron candidates are entries with abs(lep_type)==11 passing the signal-lepton mask. The official barrel/endcap crack veto is not separately resolvable beyond the provided `sig_lep` mask unless an explicit electron author/crack-veto branch is added.
open_data_particle_definitions.muons: Muon candidates are entries with abs(lep_type)==13 passing the signal-lepton mask.
open_data_particle_definitions.jets: Use jet_pt, jet_eta, jet_phi, jet_e with pT > 25 GeV and |eta| < 2.5. Apply lepton overlap removal with DeltaR(jet, selected lepton) > 0.4 in the executable workflow.
open_data_particle_definitions.b_tagged_jets: Use `jet_btag_quantile >= 4` as the open-data 70 percent b-tag working point. The continuous quantile convention is >=1 for 100 percent, >=2 for 85 percent, >=3 for 77 percent, >=4 for 70 percent, and >=5 for 60 percent.
open_data_particle_definitions.missing_transverse_momentum: Use `met` and `met_phi`; `met_mpx` and `met_mpy` are available for cross-checks.
open_data_particle_definitions.trigger: Available trigger branches are `trigE`, `trigM`, `trigMET`, `trigML`, and `TriggerMatch_DILEPTON`. In sampled files `trigML` is present but can be inactive; trigger use must be reported as an open-data approximation.
